source.description: Six-panel marker process contact sheet generated in Codex image generation and saved under drafts/beach-bucket-doodle before cropping.
source.notes: Promoted from a previously generated alternate sheet after owner requested these unused image tutorials be backfilled.

process_strategy: The same tilted bucket builds from blue construction lines to handle, shovel, sand, marker fills, shine gaps, and final outline cleanup.

final_elements.name: tilted bucket body
final_elements.description: A simple tapered bucket body with a tilted top ellipse and curved base.

final_elements.name: thick bucket rim
final_elements.description: A rounded top rim that wraps around the bucket opening.

final_elements.name: arched handle
final_elements.description: A high curved handle with side attachment circles.

final_elements.name: small sand shovel
final_elements.description: A little shovel leaning beside the bucket with a handle and scoop.

final_elements.name: bucket stripe and sand dots
final_elements.description: A horizontal stripe on the bucket and small ground dots around the base.

final_elements.name: bright marker fills
final_elements.description: Turquoise bucket color, orange handle and stripe, yellow shovel, and gray sand shadow.

final_elements.name: white shine gaps
final_elements.description: Small uncolored highlight gaps on the bucket and handle.

frames.visible_job: Places the tilted bucket body with a top ellipse, tapered sides, base curve, and center guide.
Clarifies the bucket body and adds the thick rounded rim around the opening.
Adds the arched handle and side attachment circles to the existing bucket rim.
Adds the leaning shovel, bucket stripe, small sand dots, and thick black outlines.
Adds marker fills, visible streaks, white shine gaps, and the sand shadow to existing shapes.

final_step.visible_job: Only reinforces existing black outlines, deepens marker fills, sharpens shine gaps, and strengthens the sand shadow.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
